taxonomy: Zygia gigantifoliola is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant is native to Central America, specifically in the countries of Costa Rica and Panama. It belongs to the genus Zygia, which consists of various species of legumes found in the tropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Zygia gigantifoliola places it within the subfamily Faboideae, one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies of legumes. This classification helps to group Zygia gigantifoliola with other similar species and provides a framework for understanding its evolutionary relationships.
uses: Zygia gigantifoliola has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
